vocalTraits: slightly high pitch for male speaker

telltalePatterns: Syllable-timed rhythm, lacking English stress-timed vowel reduction
Dentalization of alveolar stops (e.g., /t/ in 'little' produced as [t̪])

prosody.fluencyNotes: Fluent speech with limited use of connected speech features like linking and elision; words are often articulated in isolation.

strengths: Consistent and clear articulation of sibilants /s/ and /z/
Good control of grammar and complex sentence structures
Maintains a fluent and even pace throughout the narrative

recommendedFocus.reason: This is a high-frequency confusion that directly impacts word-level intelligibility (e.g., 'west' vs 'vest'). Mastering this is critical.
Substitution with /t/ and /d/ is a primary marker of the accent and can cause confusion with many common words (e.g., 'three' vs 'tree').
Shifting from a syllable-timed to a stress-timed pattern by reducing unstressed vowels will dramatically improve naturalness and reduce listener fatigue.

result.transcript: Yesterday the weather was really beautiful, so I decided to go for a walk through the park near my house. The trees were green and the birds were singing everywhere. I think spring is my favorite time of the year. I walked along the river and watched some children playing with a big red ball. They were laughing and having so much fun together. One little girl threw the ball very high and it landed right in the water. Her father had to reach in and pull it out. After that I stopped at a small village shop to buy some bread and a bottle of juice.
